content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Caridea; family: Pasiphaeidae; genus: Glyphus; species: marsupialis; common name: Kangaroo shrimp

title: Glyphus marsupialis
content: Large red shrimp. Carapace with dorsal ridge terminating in a short triangular rostrum. Two pairs of scissor-like chelae with fingers bearing numerous sharp, toothlike scales. Abdomen of female swollen (hence common name). Known to bioluminesce. Carnivorous.

title: Glyphus marsupialis
content: Dark red.

title: Glyphus marsupialis
content: Up to 160 mm total length.

title: Glyphus marsupialis
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Widely distributed in Pacific, Indian and (less so) Atlantic Oceans, benthic on sandy seabeds at 500-1100 m.

title: Glyphus marsupialis
content: None.

title: Glyphus marsupialis

content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Caridea; family: Pasiphaeidae; genus: Pasiphaea; species: sp. 1; common name: Glass shrimp

title: Pasiphaea sp. 1
content: Small, translucent shrimp with orange trim along dorsal and ventral carapace varying in coverage, telson, antennae and tips of chelipeds. First and second pair of pereopods chelate.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 1
content: Translucent to white, with orange colouration on edges of claws, tail and carapace, which can cover much of the body.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 1
content: Up to 90 mm body length, but usually smaller (30 mm).

title: Pasiphaea sp. 1
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 1
content: There are nine species of this genus reported from southern African waters. All are delicate shrimps with rostrum reduced or absent and first two pairs of pereopods chelate, with characteristic comb-like hairs on finger.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 2
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Caridea; family: Pasiphaeidae; genus: Pasiphaea; species: sp. 2; common name: Ventrally flattened prawn

title: Pasiphaea sp. 2
content: Ventrally flattened, very short triangle rostrum with tiny dorsal spine. Large, well-developed fine chelae on first and second pereopods. Abdominal segment two overlaps with segment one. Considerably larger and more ventrally flattened than Pasiphaea sp. 1.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 2
content: Often red thorax with white tail. Can have orange- red colouration around edges, or be completely white, or completely red to orange.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 2
content: Average 160 mm total length including rostrum.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 2
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa.

title: Pasiphaea sp. 2
content: There are nine species of this genus reported from Southern African waters. All are delicate shrimps with rostrum reduced or absent and first two pairs of pereopods chelate, with characteristic comb-like hairs on finger. Larger and more ventrally flattened than Pasiphaea sp. 1.

content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 648-650. Emmerson WD. 2016. title: Calocaris barnardi
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Axiidea; family: Axiidae; genus: Calocaris; species: barnardi; common name: Snapper shrimp

title: Calocaris barnardi
content: Resembles a sand-prawn in overall appearance. Carapace with short, horizontal, pointed rostrum lacking marginal teeth distally, but with lateral edges upturned, and with untoothed medial keel. Rostrum at base with four teeth on either side. First two pairs of pereopods chelate, the first pair much larger and more robust. Abdomen elongate and lacking ornamentation; exopod of uropod with keel. Telson longer than broad, strongly setose (with bristles) along margin, apex broadly rounded.

title: Calocaris barnardi
content: Bright to pale orange.

title: Calocaris barnardi
content: Average 80 mm total length, including claw.

title: Calocaris barnardi
content: Namibia to West Coast of South Africa.

title: Calocaris barnardi
content: None.

content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 501-503. Emmerson WD. 2016. title: Stereomastis sculpta
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Polychelida; family: Polychelidae; genus: Stereomastis; species: sculpta; common name: Deep-sea blind lobster/Sea cockroach

title: Stereomastis sculpta
content: Unusual, heavily sculptured, blind, widespread deep- sea crustacean. Carapace with median keel, lateral keels and transverse median ridge, all produced into sharp spines. Abdominal segments 1 to 5 with keels forming large forwardly-directed spines, increasing in size from first to fourth segment. Slender, elongate claws held forwards.

title: Stereomastis sculpta
content: Mostly pink with darker patches on telson, but can also be nearly all white with red patches on telson and parapodia tips.

title: Stereomastis sculpta
content: Up to 130 mm body length.

title: Stereomastis sculpta
content: Predominantly West Coast of South Africa.

title: Stereomastis sculpta
content: Polycheles typhlops has similar body shape, but is orange red and lacks spines on medial keel of carapace.

title: Stereomastis sculpta

title: Munida benguela
content: Small lobster-type crustacean. Tail often folded underneath abdomen. Carapace and abdominal segments with transverse ridges. Distinctly striped pattern on thorax. Chelipeds (claws) as long as thorax and abdomen combined.

title: Munida benguela
content: Orange-and-white striped pattern, tail white.

title: Munida benguela
content: 50-60 mm in total length.

title: Munida benguela
content: Namibia to KwaZulu-Natal, South Africa.

title: Munida benguela
content: There are 12 similar species of this genus in regional waters.

title: Dardanus arrosor
content: Unmistakable, with transverse, scaly striations on chelae and pereopods. Left cheliped larger than right one.

title: Dardanus arrosor
content: Orange to brown, eyestalks orange with two red bands.

title: Dardanus arrosor
content: Can grow to a large shield length of 75 mm, total length 250 mm. One of the largest South African hermit crabs.

title: Dardanus arrosor
content: All along South African coasts, common on South Coast shelf region, from 30-290 m.

title: Dardanus arrosor
content: Several other species of the genus Dardanus known from South Africa have similarly-coloured eyestalks and same general appearance of chelipeds, but lack the scaly striae on surface of chelae and pereopods.

title: Paguristes sp.
content: Left chela slightly larger. Chelae and pereopods with corneous tips and irregularly covered with prominent tubercles, which end in a brown, corneous spine. Not heavily covered with hairs.

title: Paguristes sp.
content: Orange, with green eyes. Tubercles on pereopods and chelae pinkish-white.

title: Paguristes sp.
content: Up to 9 mm shield length, total length 100 mm.

title: Paguristes sp.
content: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank, South Coast of South Africa, 87-126 m.

title: Paguristes sp.
content: Potentially confused with other orange hermit crabs like Sympagurus dimorphus, but members of Paguristes have relatively short, similarly-sized chelipeds.

title: Paguristes sp.
content: Species currently being described.

title: Anapagurus hendersoni
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Paguridae; genus: Anapagurus; species: hendersoni; common name: Blue-lined hermit

title: Anapagurus hendersoni
content: Enlarged right cheliped; dorsal surface of chela with small tubercles and high, spinose ridge proximally; carpus long and with row of small spines mesially. Left cheliped very slender, palm of chela (weak) and carpus with longitudinal double row of spines dorsally. Best identifiable by colouration.

title: Anapagurus hendersoni
content: Two colour morphs exist in South African waters having one of two background colourations – either cream or brownish with the same colour patterns. Shield orange to brown in both forms, corneas of eyes dark greenish to yellow-turquoise. Characteristic features are the translucent blue longitudinal stripes on the ventral margin of the propodi of the pereopods, and a maroon dot on the mesioventral (inner side) of each chela; the right a large dot and left a smaller dot (not visible in frontal view, sometimes less pronounced in the cream colour morph).

title: Anapagurus hendersoni
content: Up to 40 mm total length.

title: Anapagurus hendersoni
content: South African endemic. West Coast of South Africa to KwaZulu-Natal, 9-226 m.

title: Anapagurus hendersoni
content: Goreopagurus poorei, but A. hendersoni has distinct colour markings (blue stripes and maroon dot).

title: Anapagurus hendersoni

title: Pagurus cuanensis
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Paguridae; genus: Pagurus; species: cuanensis; common name: hairy hermit

title: Pagurus cuanensis
content: Right chela distinctively larger than left. Chelae and pereopods heavily covered with setae (hairs), usually concealing the armature. Palm of right hand with three to four rows of medium to strong spines, of which the median row is usually the strongest.

title: Pagurus cuanensis
content: Pereopods and chelae brown, completely covered with earth-coloured setae. Merus of chelipeds (see line diagram p. 136) reddish, sprinkled with whitish spots. Eyestalks yellow to orange. Second antennae reddish-brown with white rings. Offshore specimens duller in colour than inshore (False Bay) individuals; often in old and overgrown shells.

title: Pagurus cuanensis
content: Up to 8 mm shield length, total length 50 mm.

title: Pagurus cuanensis
content: Reported from Vema Seamount (Namibian West Coast), False Bay, Cape St. Blaize, Mossel Bay, Durban and KwaZulu-Natal to 130 m. Common on Agulhas Bank.

title: Pagurus cuanensis
content: Pagurus liochele, but P. cuanensis distinguished by strongly spined and very hairy chelae and does not have blue colouration of P. liochele. At least six other specimens of the genus occur in the region. Propagurus deprofundis occurs in greater depths.

title: Pagurus cuanensis
content: McLaughlin PA and Forest J. 1999. Hermit crabs of the genus Pagurus Fabricius (Crustacea, Decapoda, Paguridae) from south-eastern South Africa. Annals of the South African Museum 105: 297-344.

title: Pagurus liochele
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Paguridae; genus: Pagurus; species: liochele; common name: Blue-faced hermit

title: Pagurus liochele
content: Right chela distinctively larger than left. Palm of right hand with row of blunt spines on dorsomesial margin and surface covered with low blue tubercles. Stronger row of white-blue tubercles adjacent to cutting edge of fixed finger. Colour diagnostic. Specimens from South Coast trawls mostly in shells occupying cavities in an undescribed species of Suberites sponge (see picture).

title: Pagurus liochele
content: Eyestalks orange at base with distal half characteristically cobalt-blue. Dark purple chelipeds covered with cobalt-blue tubercles. Propodi of pereopods with cobalt-blue ring distally, dactyls with reddish longitudinal stripes. Second antennae red with white rings.

title: Pagurus liochele
content: Up to 7 mm shield length, total length 40 mm.

title: Pagurus liochele
content: Southern African endemic. Orange River to Transkei, South Africa, littoral to 110 m. Sometimes caught in inshore trawls.

title: Pagurus liochele
content: Pagurus cuanensis, however P. liochele is distinctive with blue colouration on eyestalks and propodi of walking legs. At least six other species of the genus occur in the region.

title: Pagurus liochele
content: Emmerson WD. 2016. A Guide to, and Checklist for, the Decapoda of Namibia, South Africa and Mozambique. Cambridge Scholars Publishing, Newcastle upon Tyne. Vol 2, pp. 169-172. McLaughlin PA and Forest J. 1999. Hermit crabs of the genus Pagurus Fabricius (Crustacea, Decapoda, Paguridae) from south-eastern South Africa. Annals of the South African Museum 105: 297-344.

title: Propagurus deprofundis
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Paguridae; genus: Propagurus; species: deprofundis; common name: Orange keeled hermit

title: Propagurus deprofundis
content: Chelae uniformly orange and spiny, right larger than left. Palm covered with six irregular rows of spines, accompanied by long and stiff setae. Carpus with irregular row of strong spines on dorso-mesial margin. Mesial face of propodus of second pereopod with longitudinal keel (see pictures).

title: Propagurus deprofundis
content: Pereopods, eyestalks and shield bright orange, corneas of eyes black. Tips of dactyls and fingers corneous and black.

title: Propagurus deprofundis
content: Up to 9.3 mm shield length, total length 80 mm.

title: Propagurus deprofundis
content: South Coast of South Africa. Single individuals occasionally caught in deep trawls along Agulhas Shelf. Elsewhere recorded from 200-915 m and found in variety of gastropod shells.

title: Propagurus deprofundis
content: Potentially confused with the parapagurid species Sympagurus, Parapagurus and Paragiopagurus, which can also be orange, but longitudinal keel on second walking legs and spiny chelae of P. deprofundis are distinctive. Pagurus cuanenis occurs in shallower waters.

title: Propagurus deprofundis
content: McLaughlin PA and Forest J. 1999. Hermit crabs of the genus Pagurus Fabricius (Crustacea, Decapoda, Paguridae) from south-eastern South Africa. Annals of the South African Museum 105: 297-344.

title: Goreopagurus poorei
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Paguridae; genus: Goreopagurus; species: poorei; common name: Broad-clawed hermit

title: Goreopagurus poorei
content: Immediately identifiable by very uniquely shaped, large right cheliped (even larger in males, as depicted), with carpus dorsoventrally flattened and produced to the sides, flared, with sharp spines around the inner margin. Chela long and elongated, not bearing any spines. Left cheliped slender. Eyestalks short and stout, about half the length of shield.

title: Goreopagurus poorei
content: General background colouration orange. Shield light orange, fading to white medially and near rostrum. Eyestalks mottled orange and white, distally white near black corneas. Chelipeds mostly orange with pale orange chela and fingers. Pereopods with weakly-defined orange stripe on lateral and mesial faces.

title: Goreopagurus poorei
content: Up to 60 mm in total length.

title: Goreopagurus poorei
content: Along edge of Agulhas shelf, South Africa, 334-622 m.

title: Goreopagurus poorei
content: Could be confused with Anapagurus hendersoni, which has a similar appearance, but G. poorei occurs much deeper and has a light orange colouration with black eyes. G. poorei co-occurs with Propagurus deprofundis, but is easily distinguishable from the latter by the smooth claw.

title: Goreopagurus poorei
content: Landschoff J and Lemaitre R. 2017. Crossing the Indian Ocean: a range extension for Goreopagurus poorei Lemaitre & McLaughlin, 2003 (Crustacea: Decapoda: Paguridae). Zootaxa, 4306(2): 271-278. Lemaitre R and McLaughlin PA. 2003. New species of Goreopagurus (Decapoda: Anomura: Paguridae) from Tasmania and re-evaluation of sexual tubes in hermit crab systematics. Memoirs of Museum Victoria 60(2): 221-227.

title: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Parapaguridae; genus: Paragiopagurus; species: atkinsonae; common name: Green-eyed hermit

title: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae
content: Very similar to S. dimorphus, but smaller, with same dimorphism: large right cheliped in males, in females shorter and chela rounded to oval shape. Without any obvious distinguishing characters, but overall appearance different to S. dimorphus. Pereopods longer and more slender, eyes shorter. Right cheliped not very setose. Inhabits same colonial anemone as S. dimorphus. Not recorded from gastropod shells. Distinctive colouration.

title: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae
content: More uniformly orange than S. dimorphus. Chelipeds orange, with white tubercules or spines. Segments of pereopods commonly with dorsal white spots (see arrows). Eyes usually green and eyestalks with orange pattern dorsally, not forming clear, continuous orange-red stripe, as in S. dimorphus.

title: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae
content: Shield length < 10 mm; total length up to 50 mm.

title: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae
content: South African endemic. Localised area on the West Coast of South Africa, not reported from South Coast. Known from depths 199-277 m.

title: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae
content: Sympagurus dimorphus and Parapagurus bouvieri, but distinctive colouration and green eyes of P. atkinsonae are distinguishing characters. Adults half the size of fully-grown S. dimorpus or P. bouvieri.

title: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae
content: Landschoff J and Lemaitre R. 2017. Differentiation of three common deep-water hermit crabs (Crustacea: Decapoda: Anomura: Parapaguridae) from the South African demersal abundance surveys, including the description of a new species of Paragiopagurus Lemaitre, 1996. Zookeys 676: 21-45.

title: Parapagurus andreui
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Parapaguridae; genus: Parapagurus; species: andreui; common name: Sun-anemone hermit

title: Parapagurus andreui
content: Right cheliped very large (missing in photographed specimen), and both chelipeds densely setose. Shield about as broad as long and usually well calcified. Eyestalks less than half the length of shield. Very few morphological features for identification on deck, but colour might be characteristic. Known to occupy zooanthids that have > 10 polyps arranged in a circle around the lower margin of the shell. However, a few other species might occupy the same type of zooanthid.

title: Parapagurus andreui
content: In South Africa only known from photographed specimen. Shield and bases of cephalic appendage (antennae and eyestalks) white-washed orange to mouldy white. Chelipeds appear yellowish due to heavy setation, walking legs brownish orange, colour intensified in dactyls.

title: Parapagurus andreui
content: Between 100-120 mm total length.

title: Parapagurus andreui
content: West Coast of South Africa; 731 m.

title: Parapagurus andreui
content: Parapagurus bouvieri, but P. andreui has well-calcified legs and even more densely setose chelipeds. It also occupies a different type of zooanthid.

title: Parapagurus andreui
content: Lemaitre R. 1999. Crustacea Decapoda: a review of the species of the genus Parapagurus Smith, 1879 (Parapaguridae) from the Pacific and Indian Oceans. Mémoires du Muséum National D´Histoire Naturell 180: 303-378.

title: Parapagurus bouvieri
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Parapaguridae; genus: Parapagurus; species: bouvieri; common name: hairy-clawed hermit

title: Parapagurus bouvieri
content: Both left and right cheliped densely setose, right cheliped much longer. Weakly calcified lateral faces of meri of second and third pereopods diagnostic. Exclusively inhabit pseudoshells of a single species of colonial zooanthid, which form a smooth, slimy, pinkish cloak. Zoanthid polyps arranged around bottom margin of pseudoshell (unlike evenly- distributed polyps of the epizoanthid colonising Sympagurus dimorphus and Paragiopagurus atkinsonae).

title: Parapagurus bouvieri
content: Adult with conspicuous white band along dorsal and ventral margins of pereopods. Ventral faces of pereopods orange. Chelae often with orange- pink fingertips. Some specimens have pale orange or yellow pereopods without distinctive colour patterns.

title: Parapagurus bouvieri
content: Up to 15 mm shield length, overall size up to 100 mm.

title: Parapagurus bouvieri
content: Southern African endemic. Namibia to Cape St Francis, South Africa, 63-814 m (preferred depth range 400-499 m).

title: Parapagurus bouvieri
content: Sympagurus dimorphus, but P. bouvieri chelipeds extensively covered with hair. Parapagurus andreui, but P. bouvieri has weakly calcified lateral faces of meri of pereopods.

title: Parapagurus bouvieri
content: Landschoff J and Lemaitre R. 2017. Differentiation of three common deep-water hermit crabs (Crustacea: Decapoda: Anomura: Parapaguridae) from the South African demersal abundance surveys, including the description of a new species of Paragiopagurus Lemaitre, 1996. Zookeys 676: 21-45. Lemaitre R. 1999. Crustacea Decapoda: a review of the species of the genus Parapagurus Smith, 1879 (Parapaguridae) from the Pacific and Indian Oceans. Mémoires du Muséum National D´Histoire Naturell 180: 203-378.

title: Sympagurus dimorphus
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Parapaguridae; genus: Sympagurus; species: dimorphus; common name: Dimorphic hermit

title: Sympagurus dimorphus
content: Right cheliped much larger than left. Right one sexually dimorphic (two forms), massively enlarged in male where it cannot be retracted into shell. Carpus with dorsal row of spines. Most, but not all, individuals inhabit ‘pseudoshells’ made of epizoanthids (colonial anemones). Pseudoshell coarse in texture (gritty) and light brown; > 10 orange nodules (polyps) of different sizes unevenly distributed over entire surface.

title: Sympagurus dimorphus
content: Colour variable orange-red. Chelipeds from pale orange to almost bright red, but with cream spines or tubercles. Propodus and carpus of pereopods usually with longitudinal white stripes; sometimes entirely white. Meri of chelipeds and pereopods white with orange-red patches. Eyestalks dorsally with orange-red longitudinal stripe.

title: Sympagurus dimorphus
content: Up to 30 mm shield length; overall size up to 100 mm.

title: Sympagurus dimorphus
content: Southern African endemic. Namibia to Plettenberg Bay, South Africa, 30-814 m (preferred depth range 200-249 m).

title: Sympagurus dimorphus
content: Paragiopagurus atkinsonae and Parapagurus bouvieri, but S. dimorphus has distinctly coloured pereopods.

title: Sympagurus dimorphus
content: Landschoff J and Lemaitre R. 2017. Differentiation of three common deep-water hermit crabs (Crustacea: Decapoda: Anomura: Parapaguridae) from the South African demersal abundance surveys, including the description of a new species of Paragiopagurus Lemaitre, 1996. Zookeys 676: 21-45. Lemaitre R. 2004. A worldwide review of hermit crab species of the genus Sympagurus Smith, 1883 (Crustacea: Decapoda: Parapaguridae). Tropical Deep-Sea Benthos 23: 85-149.

title: Lithodes ferox
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Lithodidae; genus: Lithodes; species: ferox; common name: Fierce king crab

title: Lithodes ferox
content: Three major pairs of pereopods visible (remaining two greatly reduced and hidden), plus chelipeds. Carapace rounded, becoming more triangular and pointed anteriorly, both gastric and cardiac regions of carapace with four prominent square-patterned spines. Rostrum strongly produced and bifid (rarely simple), with a pair of dorsal spines on corneal level. Right cheliped slightly larger, and larger in males. Chelipeds and pereopods with variously sized, strong spines.

title: Lithodes ferox
content: Bright red to pale pink or orange in colour, with reddened dactyls of pereopods.

title: Lithodes ferox
content: Up to 65-70 mm carapace width; pereopods up to 170 mm long.

title: Lithodes ferox
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa.

title: Lithodes ferox
content: Neolithodes asperrimus, but L. ferox is considerably smaller and has a long projecting double-pronged two-spined rostrum.

title: Lithodes ferox
content: Abelló P and Macpherson E. 1991. Distribution patterns and migration of Lithodes ferox (Filhol) (Anomura: Lithodidae) off Namibia. Journal of Crustacean Biology 11: 261-268. Emmerson WD. 2016. A Guide to, and Checklist for, the Decapoda of Namibia, South Africa and Mozambique. Cambridge Scholars Publishing. Vol 2, pp. 85-89.

title: Neolithodes asperrimus
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Lithodidae; genus: Neolithodes; species: asperrimus; common name: rough stone crab

title: Neolithodes asperrimus
content: Three pairs of pereopods visible (remaining two greatly reduced and hidden), plus chelipeds. Carapace with large and small spines, upper surface thickly sprinkled with small, sharp granules; gastric region of carapace with four central prominent spines in shape of a square and one additional lateral spine on each side, one single smaller spine in centre of square; cardiac area with four prominent square-patterned spines. Rostrum with one simple upward slanting spine and two dorsal spines near base. Chelipeds and pereopods thickly covered with sharp granules, more so in females than in males.

title: Neolithodes asperrimus
content: Orange.

title: Neolithodes asperrimus
content: Large; carapace width up to 200 mm; pereopods up to 500 mm long.

title: Neolithodes asperrimus
content: West Coast of South Africa and northwards to Mauritania.

title: Neolithodes asperrimus
content: Neolithodes capensis and Lithodes ferox, but this species can be differentiated by the prickly pereopods and the different spine patterns. In lithodids the length of the spinulation is highly variable depending on age.

title: Neolithodes asperrimus
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 408-413. Emmerson WD. 2016. A Guide to, and Checklist for, the Decapoda of Namibia, South Africa and Mozambique. Cambridge Scholars Publishing. Vol 2, pp. 90-93.

title: Neolithodes capensis
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Anomura; family: Lithodidae; genus: Neolithodes; species: capensis; common name: cape stone crab

title: Neolithodes capensis
content: Three pairs of pereopods visible (remaining two greatly reduced and hidden), plus chelipeds. Carapace with scattered small spines amongst larger ones. Gastric region of carapace with six prominent, hexagonally-arranged spines, cardiac region with two pairs of spines, followed by a single median one. Chelipeds and pereopods with surface somewhat smooth, scattered small and larger spines.

title: Neolithodes capensis
content: Deep brick red.

title: Neolithodes capensis
content: Large; carapace up to 200 mm wide; pereopods up to 500 mm long.

title: Neolithodes capensis
content: Endemic. West Coast of South Africa.

title: Neolithodes capensis
content: Lithodes ferox and Neolithodes asperrimus, but N. capensis lacks the distinct double-pronged projecting rostrum of L. ferox and the heavily prickly legs of N. asperrimus.

title: Neolithodes capensis
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 408-410.

title: Vitjazmaia latidactyla
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Inachidae; genus: Vitjazmaia; species: latidactyla; common name: horned eyestalk deep-water crab

title: Vitjazmaia latidactyla
content: Carapace nearly round, covered with small spines and numerous regular spinules, all curved anteriorly. Rostrum long, with long rostral spine and a pair of pseudo-rostral spines. Pseudo-rostral spines with two small spines at base of each. One very strong, long spine outside each eye, and one above each eye. Cluster of spiniform horns (2-5) visible on the eye stalk. Five pairs (chelipeds included) of very long and flat pereopods. Walking legs with surfaces covered with small, sharp spinules. Second pair of pereopods with long, sharp spines. Males with stronger and heavier tapered pincers.

title: Vitjazmaia latidactyla
content: Pale orange to peach, with darker spicules on carapace. Dactyls darker in colour.

title: Vitjazmaia latidactyla
content: Up to 150 mm carapace width.

title: Vitjazmaia latidactyla
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa, common in New Zealand and some West Indian Ocean regions.

title: Vitjazmaia latidactyla
content: Closely related to Platymaia turbynei; not to be confused with the lithodid crabs Neolithodes spp. and Lithodes ferox, which only have four instead of five fully developed pereopods (chelipeds included).

title: Vitjazmaia latidactyla
content: Naylor JR, Webber WR and Booth JD. 2005. A guide to common offshore crabs in New Zealand waters. New Zealand Aquatic Environmental and Biodiversity Report No 2, Ministry of Fisheries, Wellington, ISSN 1176-9440, 47pp. Webber R. 1995. Deep sea Majidae. Journal of the Royal Society of New Zealand, 25: 502-506.

title: Platymaia turbynei
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Inachidae; genus: Platymaia; species: turbynei; common name: Three-spined spider crab

title: Platymaia turbynei
content: Carapace rounded in shape, length slightly greater than width, surface with several small acute spines. Rostrum trispinose, with one major spine projecting forward, markedly overarching the anterior margin of carapace. Inner margin of orbit without spine. Buccal cavity with blunt denticulate tooth at outer angle. Chelipeds elongate in males. Pereopods very long and slender, second and third pereopods with long and medium-long spines, respectively, dactyls and propodi of fourth and fifth pereopods with fringes of long, fine setae along ventral margins.

title: Platymaia turbynei
content: Pink, pale orange to salmon pink; legs with very broad orange bands.

title: Platymaia turbynei
content: Carapace width up to 45 mm.

title: Platymaia turbynei
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa, Eastern Cape and KwaZulu-Natal, 200-960 m.

title: Platymaia turbynei
content: The only species of the genus recorded in South Africa, but Platymaia alcocki occurs in the Indian Ocean to Mozambique. It differs from P. turbynei by having a narrower and smoother carapace and dense hairs on the chelipeds. Platymaia longimana is reported from Namibia.

title: Platymaia turbynei
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 31-32. Emmerson WD. 2016. A Guide to, and Checklist for, the Decapoda of Namibia, South Africa and Mozambique. Cambridge Scholars Publishing. Vol 2, pp. 468-469. Griffin DJG. 1974. Spider crabs (Crustacea: Brachyura: Majidae) from the International Indian Ocean Expedition, 1963-1964. Smithsonian Contributions to Zoology 182: 1-35.

title: Achaeopsis spinulosa
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Inachidae; genus: Achaeopsis; species: spinulosa; common name: Short-spined/hotlips spider crab

title: Achaeopsis spinulosa
content: Carapace pear-shaped, broad posteriorly, tapering strongly anteriorly. Two short, stubby rostral spines extend to end of peduncle (base) apex of antennae 2. Spines slightly divergent and widely separated proximally. Strong, erect median spine on gastric region, stronger spine on cardiac region, smaller tubercle or spine on antero-lateral portion of gastric region. Total of eight spines clearly visible on dorsal carapace, similar to D. thomsoni. No visible spines at apex of fourth joint of second to fifth pereopods. Pereopods very long and slender, chelipeds rounded, bulbous.

title: Achaeopsis spinulosa
content: Orange to salmon colour when alive, with white and red mottled chelipeds. Pereopods with wide bands of orange and white (often faint).

title: Achaeopsis spinulosa
content: Up to 20 mm carapace width.

title: Achaeopsis spinulosa
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa. Widely distributed in Atlantic and Indian Oceans, usually occurring shallower than D. thomsoni.

title: Achaeopsis spinulosa
content: Rochinia hertwigi (flattened tubercles), Macropodia falcifera (more pronounced dorsal spines) and Dorhynchus thomsoni (longer rostral spines).

title: Achaeopsis spinulosa
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837, pp. 23-25.

title: Dorhynchus thomsoni
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Inachidae; genus: Dorhynchus; species: thomsoni; common name: Long-spined spider crab

title: Dorhynchus thomsoni
content: Carapace pear-shaped with strong, erect median spine on gastric region and stronger spine on cardiac region. Smaller spines on antero-lateral portion of gastric region. Total of eight spines on carapace, distinct, but not as pronounced as those of Macropodia falcifera, which has only four dorsal spines. Two rostral spines, slightly divergent distally, but close together, longer than Achaeopsis spinulosa. Rostral spines extend distinctly beyond peduncle (base) apex of antennae 2. Chelipeds more slender than A. spinulosa and rostral spines longer and closer together. Pereopods very long, with dorsodistal spine on merus of second to fifth pair (see red circles, distinguishes between D. thomsoni and A. spinulosa).

title: Dorhynchus thomsoni
content: Orange to salmon when live, with white and red mottling on chelipeds. Pereopods with wide bands of orange and white (often faint).

title: Dorhynchus thomsoni
content: Up to 20 mm carapace width.

title: Dorhynchus thomsoni
content: Predominantly West Coast, but do occur on South Coast of South Africa. Widely distributed in Atlantic and Indian Oceans, usually deeper than A. spinulosa.

title: Dorhynchus thomsoni
content: Rochinia hertwigi (flattened tubercles), Macropodia falcifera (more pronounced dorsal spines) and Achaeopsis spinulosa (shorter rostral spines).

title: Dorhynchus thomsoni
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 23-25.

title: Macropodia falcifera
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Inachidae; genus: Macropodia; species: falcifera; common name: cape long-rostrum spider crab

title: Macropodia falcifera
content: Carapace with single long erect spine in gastric region and on cardiac region, with two smaller spines on each dorso-lateral edge. Long, elongated rostrum of two sharp appressed (close together) spines, extending well beyond end of antennal peduncle (base), usually to end of flagellum of antennae 2. Eyes on long stalks, situated at distal end of extended carapace. Apex of merus of pereopods has either three, two or one small spine(s) – not to be confused with D. thomsoni, which has a single spine.

title: Macropodia falcifera
content: Pale orange to pink or red, often with darker red speckled chelipeds.

title: Macropodia falcifera
content: Carapace up to 15 mm width.

title: Macropodia falcifera
content: Widespread, West and South Coasts of South Africa, although predominantly South Coast.

title: Macropodia falcifera
content: Rochinia hertwigi (flattened tubercles), Dorhynchus thomsoni, Achaeopsis spinulosa and Macropodia formosa.

title: Macropodia falcifera
content: Emmerson WD. 2016. A Guide to, and Checklist for, the Decapoda of Namibia, South Africa and Mozambique. Cambridge Scholars Publishing, Newcastle upon Tyne. Vol 2, pp. 465-467. Ng PK, Richer De Forges B, Jones G. 2013. Taxonomy and ecology of the Cape Town Spider Crab, Macropodia falcifera (Stimpson, 1858) (Crustacea: Decapoda: Brachyura: Inachidae). Zootaxa 3626: 391-396.

title: Macropodia formosa
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Inachidae; genus: Macropodia; species: formosa; common name: cape long-legged spider crab

title: Macropodia formosa
content: Carapace pear-shaped, with single long erect spine in gastric region and another on cardiac region, plus two smaller spines on each dorso-lateral edge. Rostrum short, not extending beyond end of peduncle (base). Spines present on basal joints of antenna. Apex of merus of pereopods has either three, two or one small spine(s) present – not to be confused with D. thomsoni, which has a single spine.

title: Macropodia formosa
content: Pale orange to pink or red, often with darker red speckled chelipeds.

title: Macropodia formosa
content: Carapace up to 20 mm wide.

title: Macropodia formosa
content: South African endemic. Widespread, predominantly South Coast of South Africa to Mozambique.

title: Macropodia formosa

title: Latreillia metanesa
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Latreilliidae; genus: Latreillia; species: metanesa; common name: candycane crab

title: Latreillia metanesa
content: Very distinctive, with small, pear-shaped carapace bearing dorsal knobs. Rostrum triangular and consisting of three long spikes, one projecting medially forward and two laterally. Eyes disproportionately large and borne on extremely elongate eyestalks that are composed of two parts, a proximal slender section and a second much stouter section. Long spindly pereopods appear out of proportion to fragile body.

title: Latreillia metanesa
content: Carapace pale pink to red or orange. Pereopods vividly striped in red and white.

title: Latreillia metanesa
content: Carapace up to 15 mm length; legs up to 150 mm.

title: Latreillia metanesa
content: Pacific to Mozambique, Madagascar and South Africa.

title: Latreillia metanesa
content: Unmistakable – L. valide (not depicted) also occurs in the region and has similar striped legs, but a round carapace.

title: Latreillia metanesa
content: Emmerson WD. 2016. A Guide to, and Checklist for, the Decapoda of Namibia, South Africa and Mozambique. Cambridge Scholars Publishing. Vol 2, pp. 241-244 (notes in entry on L. valide).

title: Maja cornuta

title: Maja cornuta
content: Carapace pear-shaped, behind the post-ocular tooth four large marginal teeth, followed by one small submarginal tooth on hind part of branchial region, in the middle line three prominent spines on gastric region, rest of surface with smaller scattered spines, a pair of short spines on hind margin. Cheliped with granules on merus and carpus, fingers gaping at base in full-grown males. Body often camouflaged with attached animals. Previously called Maja capensis.

title: Maja cornuta
content: Yellow brown to orange-red or carmine.

title: Maja cornuta
content: Up to 100-150 mm carapace width.

title: Maja cornuta
content: South Coast of South Africa, from False Bay to Durban; 10-60 m depth.

title: Maja cornuta
content: None.

title: Pyromaia tuberculata
content: Carapace pear-shaped and convex, with four large tubercles, one anteriorly central and three larger ones posteriorly, each covered in small knobbly projections. Rostrum pointed, curved spine behind eye. Chelae inflated in male (shown), much more slender in female. Pereopods slender and elongate, with long dactyls.

title: Pyromaia tuberculata
content: Off-white with light brown mottled areas.

title: Pyromaia tuberculata
content: Carapace width up to 15-20 mm.

title: Pyromaia tuberculata
content: Native range is Pacific North America. Potentially introduced species to South Africa.

title: Pyromaia tuberculata
content: Superficially similar to Rochinia hertwigi, Dorhynchus thomsoni, Achaeopsis spinulosa and Macropodia falcifera, but distinguished by inflated tubercles.

title: Rochinia hertwigi
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Epialtidae; genus: Rochinia; species: hertwigi; common name: rochinia Sunday/ Two-prong crab

title: Rochinia hertwigi
content: Carapace with several distinctive flat-topped projecting tubercles. Male (left) with larger chelipeds than female (right). Carapace pear- shaped, produced anteriorly into distinctive rostrum composed of two slender, long, tapering spines, separated at their bases. Flat-topped tubercles often not well-developed, or hidden by bulbous swellings in posterior lateral regions. Chelipeds and pereopods long and slender.

title: Rochinia hertwigi
content: Pale orange – frequently covered in mud, hydroids and tunicates, etc.

title: Rochinia hertwigi
content: Male length up to 63 mm, female up to 43 mm.

title: Rochinia hertwigi
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa.

title: Rochinia hertwigi
content: Unmistakable with the flat-topped tubercles and long tapering rostral spines. Larger and more robust than Dorhynchus thomsoni, Achaeopsis spinulosa and Macropodia falcifera.

title: Rochinia hertwigi
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 50-51 (as Scyramathia hertwigi).

title: Exodromidia spinosissima
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Dromiidae; genus: Exodromidia; species: spinosissima; common name: horned baboon crab

title: Exodromidia spinosissima
content: Carapace roundly elongate, with two prominent, divergent spiniform processes projecting from front of carapace. Chelipeds long with strong chelae. Last two pereopods modified to be “carrier” legs folded behind carapace. Covered with short stiff hairs, longer bristles towards edges of carapace, chelipeds and pereopods. Chelipeds larger in males than females.

title: Exodromidia spinosissima
content: Marbled orange to brick-red with white.

title: Exodromidia spinosissima
content: Up to 18 mm carapace diameter, 20 mm length.

title: Exodromidia spinosissima
content: Endemic. West Coast of South Africa to Agulhas Bank.

title: Exodromidia spinosissima
content: Similar to Exodromidia spinosa, but with longer chelipeds and pronounced anterior spines on carapace, and also less common.

title: Exodromidia spinosissima
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. p. 327 (as E. bicornis).

title: Exodromidia spinosa
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Dromiidae; genus: Exodromidia; species: spinosa; common name: Furry baboon crab

title: Exodromidia spinosa
content: Rounded crab, especially when chelipeds are held close to body. Often covered in mud and sand and looks like a stone. Carapace convex, mottled orange to red, covered with fine short hairs and with scattered small, conical tubercles dorsally. Frontal lobes large and triangular. Lateral margin with three distinct teeth. Male chelipeds much larger and longer than female; female chelipeds often tucked under carapace. Last two pairs of pereopods reduced and folded behind carapace, occasionally used to carry sponges or ascidians.

title: Exodromidia spinosa
content: Bright orange/brick-red, mottled with white. Tips of chelipeds white.

title: Exodromidia spinosa
content: Male carapace width up to 34-35 mm, female 22-25 mm.

title: Exodromidia spinosa
content: Southern African endemic. Mainly West Coast of South Africa.

title: Exodromidia spinosa
content: Exodromidia spinosissima, which has longer, more pronounced spine-like frontal lobes. Often occurs together with Rochinia hertwigi and Dorhynchus thomsoni. Sometimes associated with Suberites sp. sponge pieces held onto dorsal carapace.

title: Exodromidia spinosa
content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. p. 326. Emmerson WD. 2016. title: Dromidia aegibotus
content: Fairly large, furry crab with domed carapace; four teeth on either side of front margin of carapace. Body and pereopods covered with dense brown coating of short hairs. Last two pairs of pereopods shortened and bent back over carapace, may carry sponge.

title: Dromidia aegibotus
content: Red with brown, mud-covered hairy layer.

title: Dromidia aegibotus
content: Carapace up to 80-90 mm wide.

title: Dromidia aegibotus
content: Endemic. South Coast of South Africa.

title: Dromidia aegibotus
content: Dromidia hirsutissima, but D. aegibotus is larger, and has shorter, stiff hairs.

title: Dromidia hirsutissima
content: Body covered with short, stiff pile and long, dense, fibrous and shaggy brown or yellow hairs. Carapace broader than long, with several teeth on front margin and one on lateral margin. Fifth pereopods not markedly shorter in length than fourth, but more slender and folded back over carapace. Typically carry a cloak of sponge or ascidian over carapace (photo on right).

title: Dromidia hirsutissima
content: Muddy brown, orange or yellow. Tips of chelipeds white.

title: Dromidia hirsutissima
content: Up to 55 mm carapace width.

title: Dromidia hirsutissima
content: Endemic. West and South Coasts of South Africa.

title: Dromidia hirsutissima
content: Exodromidia spp., but claws of D. hirsutissima folded close to mouth parts, more compact body shape and considerably longer, denser hairs.

content: Barnard KH. 1950. Descriptive catalogue of South African decapod Crustacea. Annals of the South African Museum 38: 1-837. pp. 320-321.

title: Speodromia platyarthrodes
content: Upper surface of carapace inflated into three large humps, surface studded with minute sessile, scale- like setae; frontal margin with rounded projection. Undersurface of carapace bearing unusual deep cavity thought to be associated with respiration; closed anteriorly by cheliped and posteriorly by pereopods 2-4.

content: Orange mottled with paler areas.

title: Speodromia platyarthrodes

title: Pseudodromia rotunda
content: Characterised by rounded, strongly convex carapace and unusualy elongate last pair of pereopods folded upwards to hold ascidian cloak. Two upper frontal teeth are slightly divergent, allowing the lower median tooth to be seen in dorsal view.

content: Crab inside ascidian is pale orange to peach in colour.

content: Crab almost fully enclosed by ascidian growing on dorsal carapace. Tips of chelipeds usually red and white in colour.

title: Homola barbata
content: Carapace squarish, longer than wide, prominent spines covering frontal portion of dorsal carapace, with two lateral spines projecting from front corners. Covered in short, fine orange hairs. Eyestalks long and eyes large. Last pereopods modified to fold back over carapace and often carries sponge over back. Often covered in mud.

content: Orange with paler speckles.

title: Nautilocorystes ocellatus
content: Easily recognised by the unusual elongated carapace with its rounded front armed with four sharp teeth on either side. Antennae elongated and held together by interlocking hairs to form a tube, down which water is drawn while the crab is buried in the sand.

title: Neopilumnoplax heterochir
common_name: Smooth choc-tip/ Smooth dark fingered crab
content: Relatively smooth orange-golden to brown carapace with well-defined epibranchial ridges (ridge on mid- lateral dorsal carapace). Frontal margin (rostrum) straight, with three fairly large lateral teeth behind eyes, second two pronounced and curved. Chelipeds subequal, left side slightly larger, upper surface granulate, claw fingers dark brown or black. Row of knobs along upper edge of pereopods.

title: Monodaeus sp.
content: Carapace orange- to red-speckled, nodular and marked with distinct grooves running back from anterior edge. Four blunt spines projecting from lateral edge to just before eye, posterior spines have white tips. Rostrum square with no projections. Pereopods hairy and frequently coated in mud. Chelae subequal, ends of finger and hand black.

title: Monodaeus sp.
content: Orange-red-brown speckled, with paler portions of body, fingers black.

title: Monodaeus sp.
content: Usually ± 40 mm diameter carapace width.

title: Monodaeus sp.

title: Chaceon chuni

title: Chaceon chuni
content: Large orange crab, sometimes with black mottled carapace. Carapace quadrangular, smooth, lateral margin with five teeth on each side of eye, second and fourth smaller. Pereopods long and unmodified. Dactyls of fifth pereopod laterally flattened (from sides). Commonly caught in trawl nets in large numbers (> 100).

title: Chaceon chuni
content: Bright orange, sometimes with black mottled colouration.

title: Chaceon chuni
content: Average 80 mm width, 68 mm length. Maximum recorded: 138 mm width, 122 mm length.

title: Chaceon chuni
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa between 300-1 400 m depth.

title: Chaceon chuni
content: Chaceon macphersoni and Chaceon maritae, however these are paler than C. chuni, which is generally smaller in size, has a smoother carapace and flattened dactyl of fifth pereopod.

title: Chaceon chuni

title: Chaceon macphersoni
content: phylum: Arthropoda; subphylum: Crustacea; class: Malacostraca; order: Decapoda; infraorder: Brachyura; family: Geryonidae; genus: Chaceon; species: macphersoni; common name: White-leg crab

title: Chaceon macphersoni
content: Large crab, often co-occurring with Chaceon chuni in trawl catches. Clearly distinguished by the white pereopods with orange tips and orange blotches of shading on carapace. Carapace is granular in texture and has characteristic markings. Lateral margin with five teeth on each side of eye, second and fourth smaller. Tips of pereopods are dorso- ventrally flattened (from top to bottom).

title: Chaceon macphersoni
content: White pereopods with orange tips and orange shading on carapace, no orange tips on chelae.

title: Chaceon macphersoni
content: Average 80 mm carapace width (on average larger than C. chuni), but recorded up to 150 mm carapace width.

title: Chaceon macphersoni
content: Southern African endemic. West and South Coasts of South Africa; 250-900 m depth.

title: Chaceon macphersoni
content: Chaceon chuni and Chaceon maritae, but distinguished by very white pereopods, orange dactyl tips and granulated carapace.

title: Chaceon maritae
content: Similar in appearance to other Chaceon species, having five teeth on each side of eye, second and fourth smaller or obsolete. Tips of pereopods are dorso-ventrally flattened (from top to bottom). Carapace can be granulated in frontal portion.

title: Chaceon maritae
content: Pale orange to yellow.

title: Chaceon maritae
content: Average carapace width 95 mm, reported up to 131 mm.

title: Chaceon maritae
content: From Agulhas Bank along Atlantic coast into North- West Africa forming part of an important fishery; between 100 and 900+ m depth.

content: Chaceon chuni and C. macphersoni, but C. maritae has dorso-ventrally flattened dactyls of pereopods and different colouration to C. macphersoni.

title: Macropipus australis
content: Carapace with three frontal and four sharp lateral spines, and one lateral spine projecting horizontally. Has distinct symmetrical white markings against deep red colouration, giving a painted appearance. Fifth pair of pereopods modified as swimming paddles.

title: Macropipus australis
content: Brick red to maroon colouration with white markings.

title: Macropipus australis

title: Ovalipes iridescens
content: Carapace with three sharp teeth between eyes, broad orbital notch and then five lateral teeth to side, dorsal surface finely granulated except two paler oval membranous areas posteriorly. Chelipeds two to five distinct spines on upper surface. Last pair of pereopods modified for swimming.

title: Ovalipes iridescens
content: Red markings on paler yellowish background, chelae white-tipped. Iridescent, particularly on chelipeds and carapace.

title: Ovalipes iridescens
content: Up to 80 mm carapace width.

title: Ovalipes iridescens
content: South and East Coasts of South Africa, to eastern Pacific.

title: Ovalipes trimaculatus
content: Carapace pink, finely granulate, front with four teeth between eyes, a tooth on upper orbital margin and four strong teeth on antero-lateral margin behind outer orbital tooth, three distinctive red marks – a central curved mark and two dots on posterior corners. Last pair of pereopods modified for swimming. Formerly known as Ovalipes punctatus.

title: Ovalipes trimaculatus
content: Creamy grey or pale buff, speckled with reddish dots, a median crescentric red mark and an oval red spot near each postero-lateral corner.

title: Ovalipes trimaculatus
content: Up to 80-100 mm carapace width.

title: Ovalipes trimaculatus
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa (and widespread around Southern Hemisphere).

common_name: red and white legged swimming crab
content: Carapace oval, with scalloped ridge running horizontally across centre. Four rounded projections across front of carapace. title: Atelecyclus rotundatus
common_name: round sand crab/Old man's face crab
content: Carapace rounded, surface granular, thickly setose (with bristles) around margins and anteriorly around mouthparts; tridentate between eyes, lateral margin with about 10 serrate teeth. Chelipeds equal, large and powerful, held closely up against front of body, strongly setose dorsally. Chela with horizontal lines of granules, fingers darker. Pereopods short, setose around margins and granular.

common_name: round sand crab/Old man's face crab

title: Mursia cristiata
content: Carapace roughly oval, pale orange with bright red tubercles. Front lateral edges of carapace crenulate, with about 10 small teeth, followed by a much larger, sharp spine projecting laterally. Chelipeds broad and strongly spinose, mostly held close to the mouth, hence the name ‘masked’ crab.

title: Goneplax clevai
content: Smooth, quadrangular carapace, wider than long and with two strong forward-directed lateral teeth. Colour pattern distinctive, marked with distinct scalloped line approximately midway across carapace. Front portion of carapace darker brown, rear half lighter brown. Pereopods long, male has much longer chelipeds than female (female depicted). Previously known as Gonoplax rhomboides, but South African material described as distinct new species by Guinot and Castro (2007).

title: Goneplax clevai
content: Red and orange – darker red patterned line across carapace, posterior part of carapace and pereopods paler.

title: Hornera erugata
content: Erect, delicately calcified and branching. Forms curved tree-like branches with secondary branches emanating from a central tubular main branch. Tubular zooids protrude from the frontal surface (usually facing away from substratum), while the basal surface is smooth in this species. Commonly epizoic on other bryozoans and hard substrata.

title: Hornera erugata
content: Off-white.

title: Hornera erugata
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Hornera erugata
content: Endemic. Occur at depths of 35-90 m on the West, South and East Coasts of South Africa.

content: Erect or semi-erect, flexible, fleshy/gelatinous mass of lobes. Zooids can be found on both sides of the lobes and have a rhomboid shape.

content: Yellow to brown.

content: Colony may be 150 mm in diameter.

title: Alcyonidium rhomboidale
content: Endemic. West Coast from north of Cape Columbine to the South Coast, Agulhas Bank. From 5 m to 400 m depth.

title: Flustramorpha marginata
content: Erect, forming lightly calcified, flexible, narrow, strappy fronds that branch dichotomously. Margins and internodes on the face of the fronds are thickened, attached to substrate by a holdfast.

title: Flustramorpha marginata
content: Blue-green.

title: Flustramorpha marginata
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Flustramorpha marginata
content: Endemic. West Coast from False Bay to South Coast, Algoa Bay in South Africa. From 29 m to 450 m depth.

title: Flustramorpha marginata
content: F. angusta and Securiflustra securifrons may appear similar, but F. marginata is distinguished by its blue- green colour.

title: Flustramorpha marginata
content: Hayward PJ and Cook PL. 1983. The South African Museum’s Meiring Naudé Cruises. Part 13, Bryozoa II. Annals of the South African Museum 91: 1–161.

title: Flustramorpha angusta
content: Similar to F. marginata: erect, forming lightly calcified, flexible, narrow, strappy fronds that branch dichotomously. However, this species is less robust and cream to light brown in colour. Margins and internodes on the face of the fronds are thickened, attached to substrate by a holdfast.

title: Flustramorpha angusta
content: Cream to light brown.

title: Flustramorpha angusta
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Flustramorpha angusta
content: Endemic. West Coast of South Africa to northern KwaZulu-Natal from 17 m to 780 m depth.

title: Flustramorpha angusta
content: F. marginata and Securiflustra securifrons may appear similar, but F. angusta is distinguished by being more fragile, with thickened margins and its cream-brown colour.

title: Flustramorpha angusta
content: Hayward PJ and Cook PL. 1983. The South African Museum’s Meiring Naudé Cruises. Part 13, Bryozoa II. Annals of the South African Museum 91: 1–161.

title: Securiflustra sp. 1
content: Erect, forming lightly calcified, flexible, narrow, strappy fronds that branch dichotomously, having paper-thin blades that are yellow to brown in colour. Attach to substrate by a holdfast.

title: Securiflustra sp. 1
content: Yellow to brown.

title: Securiflustra sp. 1
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Securiflustra sp. 1
content: Recorded from the South Coast of South Africa at a depth of 72 m but may have greater depth range. The South African specimens appear to be consistent with the genus Securiflustra, which is reported to be endemic to Europe. Taxonomy of this species is uncertain and specimens must be retained.

title: Securiflustra sp. 1
content: Similar in apperance to Flustramorpha species. F. marginata and F. angusta may appear similar, but Securiflustra is distinguished by paper-thin blades with no marginal thickening and its yellow colour. F. marginata is blue-green and F. angusta is cream to light brown.

title: Securiflustra sp. 1
content: Hayward PJ and Cook PL. 1983. The South African Museum’s Meiring Naudé Cruises. Part 13, Bryozoa II. Annals of the South African Museum 91: 1–161.

content: Erect form, lightly calcified tree-like colony that may or may not have thin branches arranged in a spiral whorl-like pattern.

title: Menipea triseriata
content: Yellow to pale orange.

title: Menipea triseriata
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Menipea triseriata
content: Endemic. West, South and East Coasts of South Africa from shallow subtidal to 287 m depth.

content: Menipea ornata is a similar species (not depicted in this guide) with broader branches and is more robust. Specimens should be retained.

content: Erect form, lightly calcified tree-like colony, easily recognisable by its inward-curving branches and yellow to brown colour.

title: Menipea crispa
content: Tan to brown.

title: Menipea crispa
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Menipea crispa
content: Endemic. West, South and East Coasts of South Africa from shallow subtidal to 400 m depth.

content: M. ornata, M. triseriata and M. marionensis, but M. crispa is distinguished by inward-curling branches.

title: Menipea marionensis
content: Erect form, distinctly tree-like colony that is more delicate than other Menipea species, having finer spirally arranged branches. Colour is tan to cream or white.

title: Menipea marionensis
content: Tan to pale white.

title: Menipea marionensis
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Menipea marionensis
content: Endemic. Found in waters of the West Coast of South Africa to just south of East London. Depth range from 55 to 400 m.

title: Menipea marionensis
content: M. triseriata, M. ornata and M. crispa similar, but M. marionensis has finer branching and a distinctly tree-like shape.

title: Onchoporella buskii
content: Erect, forming flexible and very lightly calcified fronds that are strap-like and translucent. Zooids are convex, giving the branches a scaly appearance on one side of branches only.

title: Onchoporella buskii
content: Fronds translucent to tan.

title: Onchoporella buskii
content: Colony may be 100-150 mm in diameter.

title: Onchoporella buskii
content: West and South Coasts of South Africa, Namibia to Port Elizabeth from shallow subtidal to 400 m depth.

title: Turbicellepora valligera
content: Erect, but originates from an encrusting base which develops into tapered cylindrical branches that are heavily calcified and branch dichotomously. Resembles stag-horn coral.

title: Turbicellepora valligera
content: Off-white to light orange, but sometimes with a green tinge.

title: Turbicellepora valligera
content: Branches may be 50-100 mm in length.

title: Turbicellepora valligera
content: Endemic. West Coast, Port Nolloth to the East Coast of South Africa. Depth range from 2 to 278 m.

title: Turbicellepora valligera
content: Can be distinguished from Adeonella spp. by its cylindrical branches. Adeonella have flattened strap- like branches.

content: Florence WK, Hayward PJ and Gibbons MJ. 2007. Taxonomy of shallow-water Bryozoa from the west coast of South Africa. African Natural History 3: 1–58.

title: Adeonella spp.
content: phylum: Bryozoa; class: Gymnolaemata; order: Cheilostomatida; family: Adenellidae; genus: Adeonella; species: spp.; common name: Sabre bryozoan

title: Adeonella spp.
content: Erect, brittle, forming calcified, flattened, strap-like colonies that branch dichotomously. Branches may fuse to form coral-like structures. Zooids are large enough to be visible on both sides of straps. Often mistaken for Stylaster hydrozoans. Not flexible and has sandpapery texture.

title: Adeonella spp.
content: Mainly white, but some species may be tan to light brown in colour.

title: Adeonella spp.
content: Colonies may be anything from 50-200 mm in length.

title: Adeonella spp.
content: Most species endemic to South Africa. Found in waters of the West, South and East Coasts of South Africa. Depth range from shallow subtidal to 880m.

title: Adeonella spp.
content: Species of this genus are weakly characterised and difficult to identify beyond the generic level; even when using zooidal characters.

title: Adeonella spp.
content: Florence WK, Hayward PJ and Gibbons MJ. 2007. Taxonomy of shallow-water Bryozoa from the west coast of South Africa. African Natural History 3: 1–58.

content: Erect or encrusting, forming large twisted masses of fused, heavily calcified plates that resemble plated corals.

title: Laminopora jellyae
content: Dark to light brown in colour, sometimes with a greenish tinge.

title: Laminopora jellyae
content: Colonies may be 100-300 mm in diameter.

title: Laminopora jellyae
content: Endemic. West Coast, False Bay to East London. Depth range from 15 to 147 m.

title: Laminopora jellyae
content: No obvious similar species known.

content: Florence WK, Hayward PJ and Gibbons MJ. 2007. Taxonomy of shallow-water Bryozoa from the west coast of South Africa. African Natural History 3: 1–58.

content: phylum: Bryozoa; class: Gymnolaemata; order: Cheilostomatida; family: Chaperiidae; genus: Chaperiopsis; species: multifida; common name: Furry bryozoan

title: Chaperiopsis multifida
content: Erect, but originates from an encrusting base which develops into a series of folded erect plates. Zooids are found on both sides. Colony appears furry on the surface because of several branched spines that cover zooids.

title: Chaperiopsis multifida
content: Dark red to maroon or dusky pink.

title: Chaperiopsis multifida
content: Colonies may be 100-150 mm in diameter.

title: Chaperiopsis multifida
content: Endemic to South Africa. West Coast of South Africa to East Coast, Durban from shallow subtidal to 375 m.

title: Chaperiopsis multifida
content: Laminopora jellyae also form folded, erect plates but these are smooth in texture compared to those of C. multifida, which are “furry” and more textured.

content: Florence WK, Hayward PJ and Gibbons MJ. 2007. Taxonomy of shallow-water Bryozoa from the west coast of South Africa. African Natural History 3: 1–58

title: Aspidostoma sp. 1
content: Erect colonies, forming plates sometimes with perforations that are irregular in shape and size. Some specimens may not have perforations. Zooids can be seen on both sides of plates. Usually collected as fragments.

title: Aspidostoma sp. 1
content: Deep red to maroon.

title: Aspidostoma sp. 1
content: Fragmentary; intact colony size unknown.

content: South Coast, Agulhas Bank, South Africa from 90 to 780 m.

content: Aspidostoma livida is deep blue in colour and plates have large perforations irregular in shape.

title: Phidoloporidae spp.
content: Erect, forming coral-like mass with folded plates that are often regularly perforated, giving the colony a honeycomb appearance.

title: Phidoloporidae spp.
content: Off-white or cream to orange.

title: Phidoloporidae spp.
content: Colonies may be 50–200 mm in diameter.

title: Phidoloporidae spp.
content: Found between depths of 2-775 m on the West, South and East Coasts of South Africa.

title: Phidoloporidae spp.
content: There are many genera and species in the family Phidoloporidae that have the characteristic honeycomb-plated morphology that is coral-like. Reteporella lata (depicted left) is cream in colour with robust perforated plates. Schizoretepora tessellata (depicted right) is orange in colour and may or may not have pores, which appear to be a plastic feature related to environmental pressures.

title: Megerlina capensis
content: phylum: Brachiopoda; class: Rhynchonellata; order: Terebratulida; family: Kraussinidae; genus: Megerlina; species: capensis; common name: Common name ribbed Lamp shell

title: Megerlina capensis
content: Small rounded sub-pentagonal to sub-quadrate shells with length and width about equal. Ventral valve (shell) slightly deeper than dorsal valve. Anterior commissure (line along which the valves meet, viewed from the anterior) gently sulcate (i.e. with a broad U-shaped deflection). Relatively large pedicle opening bounded laterally by small, flat, triangular inter-areas. Fine concentric growth lines and 24-33 rounded radial ribs visible exteriorly from the 5-mm growth stage.

title: Megerlina capensis
content: Usually pinkish or reddish but may be white or cream, sometimes with red margins.

title: Megerlina capensis
content: Usually not more than 15 mm in length.

title: Megerlina capensis
content: West, South and East Coasts of South Africa.

title: Megerlina capensis
content: Looks most like the shallower water form Kraussina rubra (Pallas, 1766) but this can be distinguished by its larger size and coarser ribbing. Specimens frequently have the posterior end abraded by close attachment to a rocky substrate resulting in enlargement of the pedicle opening.

title: Megerlina capensis

title: Xenobrochus sp.
content: Small, elongate oval, strongly biconvex shells. Ventral valve (shell) deeper than dorsal valve. Anterior commissure (line along which the valves meet, viewed from the anterior) straight (rectimarginate). Pedicle opening small, sub-circular. Shell surface smooth except for fine concentric growth lines. Shell material very thin.

title: Xenobrochus sp.
content: White.

title: Xenobrochus sp.
content: Usually around 11 or 12 mm in length.

title: Xenobrochus sp.
content: West, South and East coasts of South Africa.

title: Xenobrochus sp.
content: The small size and smooth shell readily distinguishes this species from most others known in South African waters apart from others in the genus. Specimen shown in photograph on this page most likely Xenobrochus agulhasensis. Gryphus capensis Jackson, 1952 (not shown in this guide) is superficially similar but differs in the form of the internal structures of the dorsal valve.

title: Calliotropis granolirata
content: Shell small, with conical spire and rounded base; sculptured by strong spiral cords bearing well- developed granules; spire whorls with three cords above and including periphery; base with four cords; umbilicus closed; aperture nacreous (mother-of- pearl) when fresh.

title: Calliotropis granolirata
content: Uniformly milky-white to pale buff, lustreless.

title: Calliotropis granolirata
content: Length (height) up to 13 mm.

title: Calliotropis granolirata
content: South African endemic. To date known reliably only from deep water off the Cape Agulhas–Cape Point region, to depths of 2 750 m. More accurate locality data is urgently needed.

title: Calliotropis granolirata
content: None on Agulhas Bank.

title: Calliostoma perfragile
content: phylum: Mollusca; class: Gastropoda; subclass: Vetigastropoda; order: Trochida; family: Calliostomatidae; genus: Calliostoma; species: perfragile; common name: Agulhas calliostoma

title: Calliostoma perfragile
content: Shell top-shaped, with conical spire and somewhat flattened base; spire whorls slightly convex, suture shallowly indented; periphery roundly angular, but not keeled; sculptured by spiral cords of which the first two to three below suture are finely granular, the others smooth; cord intervals often with a fine spiral thread; base smoother with several broad spiral cords around umbilical region; umbilicus closed; aperture nacreous (mother-of-pearl); operculum circular, multi-spiral.

title: Calliostoma perfragile
content: Spire overall pale orange-brown (biscuit-coloured), rather glossy and slightly iridescent; under microscope spiral cords whitish, their intervals orange-brown; periphery with a spiral row of dash- like brown markings; base paler.

title: Calliostoma perfragile
content: Length up to 25 mm.

title: Calliostoma perfragile
content: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (Cape canyon to southern Transkei), perhaps also KwaZulu-Natal, 100-350 m.

title: Calliostoma perfragile
content: Calliostoma ornatum, a shallower water species from the Cape south coast, lacks the peripheral brown markings of C. perfragile. The east coast C. scotti is much larger and has more strongly angled periphery and concave spire.

title: Turritella declivis
content: phylum: Mollusca; class: Gastropoda; subclass: Caenogastropoda; order: unassigned Caenogastropoda; family: Turritellidae; genus: Turritella; species: declivis; common name: Zebra turret shell/ Bokhoring

title: Turritella declivis
content: Shell long and slender, whorls flattened or slightly concave (hollowed inwards); base of last whorl angular; aperture small and slightly flaring at base; surface with fine, curved axial growth-lines, becoming obsolete on lower part of each whorl; no spiral sculpture; outer lip thin, often damaged.

title: Turritella declivis
content: Shell cream-coloured with a broad brown mid-whorl spiral band; shell surface sometimes etched and colour indistinct; juveniles with brown spots below suture.

title: Turritella declivis
content: Length up to 100 mm, but usually less than 65 mm.

title: Turritella declivis
content: South African endemic. Common on the Agulhas Bank (Kei River to False Bay), in places hugely abundant and dominating the marine benthos; also found on West Coast, but evidently in much lower numbers (more specimens needed to confirm its distribution on West Coast).

title: Turritella declivis
content: Turritella carinifera has a distinct mid-whorl spiral keel and is whitish to buff, lilac or pale mauve-brown, lacking the distinctive brown spiral band of T. declivis.

title: Turritella declivis
content: Herbert DG. 2013. Turritella declivis Adams & Reeve, in Reeve, 1849 (Mollusca: Gastropoda) – a South African not an Australian species, and a characteristic component of the Agulhas Bank benthos. African Zoology 48(2): 412–417. http:// dx.doi.org/10.3377/004.048.0206. Steyn DG and Lussi M. 2005. Offshore shells of southern Africa: A pictorial guide to more than 750 gastropods. Published by the authors. p. 16.

title: Turritella ferruginea
content: phylum: Mollusca; class: Gastropoda; subclass: Caenogastropoda; order: unassigned Caenogastropoda; family: Turritellidae; genus: Turritella; species: ferruginea; common name: Speckled turret shell

title: Turritella ferruginea
content: Shell relatively large, many-whorled, long and slender, tapering gradually toward apex; whorls slightly convex (rounded outward), sculptured with numerous close-set, crisp, spiral threads; surface dull; basal angle distinct, delineated by a stronger spiral cord (arrowed in figure); aperture rounded; outer lip distinctly concave (hollowed inwards).

title: Turritella ferruginea
content: Cream to buff, speckled with reddish-brown, sometimes in the form of curved axial flames.

title: Turritella ferruginea
content: Length up to 110 mm, occasionally more.

title: Turritella ferruginea
content: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (False Bay to Algoa Bay), 40–210 m.

title: Turritella ferruginea
content: Might be confused with Turritella sanguinea, but in that species the whorls are more convex, the spiral sculpture more rounded, and the basal angle is not delineated by a slightly stronger spiral cord.

title: Turritella ferruginea
content: Barnard KH. 1963. Contributions to the knowledge of South African marine Mollusca. Part III. Gastropoda: Prosobranchiata: Taenioglossa. Annals of the South African Museum 47(1): 1–199. p. 174.

title: Turritella sanguinea
content: phylum: Mollusca; class: Gastropoda; subclass: Caenogastropoda; order: unassigned Caenogastropoda; family: Turritellidae; genus: Turritella; species: sanguinea; common name: Mottled turret shell

title: Turritella sanguinea
content: Shell relatively large, many-whorled, long and slender, tapering gradually toward apex; whorls convex (rounded outward), sculptured with relatively uniform rounded or flat-topped spiral cords; surface dull; basal angle not delineated by a stronger spiral cord; aperture rounded; outer lip shallowly concave (hollowed inwards).

title: Turritella sanguinea
content: Cream to buff with reddish-brown dashes on the spiral cords, sometimes aligned into axial flames or bands.

title: Turritella sanguinea
content: Length up to 100 mm, occasionally more.

title: Turritella sanguinea
content: South African endemic. Agulhas Bank (False Bay to East London) and extending northwards into KwaZulu- Natal (the smaller T. salisburyi form), 30–120 m.

title: Turritella sanguinea
content: Might be confused with Turritella ferruginea, but that species has less strongly convex whorls, finer, crisper spiral sculpture, and the basal angle is stronger and delineated by a slightly larger spiral cord.

title: Turritella sanguinea
content: Barnard KH. 1963. Contributions to the knowledge of South African marine Mollusca. Part III. Gastropoda: Prosobranchiata: Taenioglossa. Annals of the South African Museum 47(1): 1–199. p. 169. Steyn DG and Lussi M. 2005. Offshore shells of southern Africa: A pictorial guide to more than 750 gastropods. Published by the authors. p. 16.
